Satheesan, on Wednesday formally moved to dismantle the controversial Silver Line semi high-speed rail project, announcing the cancellation of all land acquisition notifications issued for the project across Kerala. results.title: New ‘Mononoke’ Movie Trailer Recaps the Trilogy’s Haunting Buildup
results.description: SummaryTwin Engine dropped a three-minute trailer for the final Mononoke filmThe footage recaps the trilogy while building a haunting mood for the finaleDirected by Kenji Nakamura, the avant-garde film hits Japanese theaters May 29, 2026Twin Engine has unveiled an extensive new three-minute special trailer for Mononoke the Movie: Chapter 3 - The Serpent God, offering a much deeper, more haunting look into the final part of the theatrical trilogy.Following the previous teaser, this latest footage serves as both a recap of the journey so far and a hauntingly atmospheric buildup, setting a profound mood by mirroring the narrative progression established across the last two films. Directed by Kenji Nakamura and produced by Twin Engine, the film pushes the franchise's legendary, avant-garde "Ukiyo-e" paper-texture animation to an unprecedented cinematic scale.Since emerging as a cult-classic spin-off in 2007, Mononoke has achieved massive global popularity for its unique blend of psychological horror, traditional folklore and vibrant, kaleidoscope visuals. With the domestic Japanese theatrical release officially set for May 29, 2026, this final chapter is heavily poised to deliver a definitive, visually unparalleled conclusion to the Medicine Seller's journey before transitioning to international audiences via a global streaming rollout. Read more at Hypebeast

results.title: Leaving your SSD in the front panel ruins its speed and lifespan
results.description: If you own an external SSD, you’re already ahead of the curve when it comes to following the 3-2-1 rule for backups. But while using an external SSD is as easy as just plugging it in and drag-and-dropping some files, you might be hindering its performance by plugging it in at the front. Read More

results.title: Middle East crisis live: US and Iran trade threats but Trump insists Tehran wants deal
results.description: Trump says he could strike Iran again as Tehran threatens ‘new fronts’ in war if an attack takes place Trump threatens ‘a big hit’ if Tehran does not make deal soon Welcome to our live coverage of events in the Middle East. Donald Trump warned the US may strike Iran again – a day after he said he had held off a major assault in hopes of a peace deal – but Tehran’s army threatened to open “new fronts” if he went ahead. The US Senate has advanced a war-powers resolution that would end the Iran war unless Trump obtains Congress’ authorisation . The vote on a procedural measure to advance the resolution was 50 to 47, as four of Trump’s fellow Republicans voted with every Democrat but one in favour. Three Republicans missed the vote. The Israeli military launched a series of strikes across Lebanon, killing 19 people , according to the Lebanese health ministry. One strike, in the town of Deir Qanun al-Nahr in the Tyre district, killed 10 people including three children and three women , the ministry said. The Israeli army in turn said that it intercepted a drone fired from Lebanon. Israel and Lebanon’s central government have twice extended a US-brokered ceasefire, but Israel says it does not apply to its attacks on Hezbollah. Israeli authorities said 430 activists aboard a Gaza-bound aid flotilla were taken to Israel after their vessels were intercepted in international waters near Cyprus. Sailing from Turkey last week, the Global Sumud Flotilla is the latest in a string of attempts by activists to breach Israel’s blockade of the Palestinian territory, with the last convoy intercepted by Israeli forces last month. Russia’s deputy foreign minister, Sergei Ryabkov, said Moscow was ready to help with talks between the US and Iran to end the war, according to the Russian Tass news agency. His remarks came as the Russian president, Vladimir Putin, held talks with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in Beijing. The United Arab Emirates was rattled by a drone attack on its Barakah nuclear power plant last week. On Tuesday the UAE said it originated from Iraqi territory, where Iran backs groups accused of launching attacks on Gulf nations in the war. The UN Security Council on Tuesday condemned the attack. Russia, which often defends Iran, joined the other members. Two Chinese tankers laden with oil exited the strait of Hormuz on Wednesday , according to shipping data. Continue reading...
